{"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "planned", "component_conditions": [], "percent_complete": 0, "value": 4243, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Calculated tension in each sling leg is approximately 4,243 lb, exceeding the 4,000 lb tagged WLL of each leg.", "Sling angle factor at 45° increases leg tension to about 70.7% of load per leg, overloading the rigging."], "actions": ["Select slings with a higher WLL or increase the sling angle to reduce leg tension below 4,000 lb per leg.", "Verify rigging selection with engineered lift plan and ASME B30.9/OSHA 1926.251 requirements before lift."], "rationale": "For a two-leg bridle with equal legs and a horizontal sling angle of 45°, the tension in each leg equals one-half the load divided by sin(45°): 6,000 / (2 × 0.7071) ≈ 4,243 lb. This exceeds the 4,000 lb WLL of each leg, so the arrangement is not acceptable.", "references": ["ASME B30.9 Slings", "OSHA 1926.251 Rigging equipment for material handling"]}
